item.text: I’m checking the current VCS/test state in the IDE first so I can align the implementation with the patched REST tests and avoid clobbering any prior agent changes.

arguments.reason: Build REST endpoints under /api for owners, pets, and visits that expose CRUD plus the tested behavior: owners list/search/paginate/get/create/update/delete; pets nested under owners for create/list and by ID for get/update/delete with duplicate-name conflict and Location on create; visits nested under pets and by ID with non-empty description validation and Location on create; return 404 for missing parents/resources and validate via bean validation. First, check IDE/project readiness, build environment, docker availability, VCS-modified files, and inspect the patched REST controller tests that define expected behavior.
arguments.code:
println("Project: ${project.name}, base: ${project.basePath}")
val dockerOk = java.io.File("/var/run/docker.sock").exists()
println("Docker: $dockerOk")
val mavenBin = "/opt/idea/plugins/maven/lib/maven3/bin/mvn"
println("Maven: ${if (java.io.File(mavenBin).exists()) mavenBin else "NOT FOUND"}")
val gradlew = java.io.File(project.basePath + "/gradlew")
println("Gradlew: ${if (gradlew.exists()) gradlew.absolutePath else "NOT FOUND"}")
val mvnw = java.io.File(project.basePath + "/mvnw")
println("Mvnw: ${if (mvnw.exists()) mvnw.absolutePath else "NOT FOUND"}")
val jvmDir = java.io.File("/usr/lib/jvm")
println("JDKs: ${jvmDir.listFiles()?.map { it.name }?.filter { it.startsWith("temurin") }?.joinToString(", ") ?: "none"}")
println("Current JAVA_HOME: ${System.getProperty("java.home")}")
val changes = readAction {
    com.intellij.openapi.vcs.changes.ChangeListManager.getInstance(project)
        .allChanges.mapNotNull { it.virtualFile?.path }.sorted()
}
println("VCS-modified files:\n" + changes.joinToString("\n"))

import com.intellij.openapi.vfs.VfsUtil
import com.intellij.openapi.vfs.LocalFileSystem

val filesToRead = listOf(
    "src/test/java/org/springframework/samples/petclinic/owner/OwnerRestControllerTests.java",
    "src/test/java/org/springframework/samples/petclinic/owner/PetRestControllerTests.java",
    "src/test/java/org/springframework/samples/petclinic/owner/VisitRestControllerTests.java"
)
for (path in filesToRead) {
    val vf = LocalFileSystem.getInstance().findFileByPath(project.basePath + "/" + path)
    if (vf == null) {
        println("NOT FOUND: $path")
        continue
    }
    val text = VfsUtil.loadText(vf)
    println("===== FILE: $path =====")
    if (text.isEmpty()) {
        println("EMPTY: $path")
    } else {
        println(text)
    }
}

All method names are compliant * with Spring Data naming conventions so this interface can easily be extended for Spring * Data. See: * https://docs.spring.io/spring-data/jpa/docs/current/reference/html/#repositories.query-methods.query-creation * * @author Ken Krebs * @author Juergen Hoeller * @author Sam Brannen * @author Michael Isvy * @author Wick Dynex */ public interface OwnerRepository extends JpaRepository { /** * Retrieve {@link Owner}s from the data store by last name, returning all owners * whose last name starts with the given name. * @param lastName Value to search for * @return a Collection of matching {@link Owner}s (or an empty Collection if none * found) */ Page findByLastNameStartingWith(String lastName, Pageable pageable); /** * Retrieve an {@link Owner} from the data store by id. *

* This method returns an {@link Optional} containing the {@link Owner} if found. If * no {@link Owner} is found with the provided id, it will return an empty * {@link Optional}. *

* @param id the id to search for * @return an {@link Optional} containing the {@link Owner} if found, or an empty * {@link Optional} if not found. * @throws IllegalArgumentException if the id is null (assuming null is not a valid * input for id) */ Optional findById(@Nonnull Integer id); }
